title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_103 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 103 the late high-barrier F25 wrapper still survives beyond the preserved p <= 97 window: keep A at the fixed A0 reservoir, keep B = {1,293,307,311}, retain the 96-label complementary-support window C0, and add exactly six extra support-safe labels 295,301,305,313,317,319 to reach |C| = 102.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_103 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. Its real campaign value is not a proved new family theorem but a verified post-97 feeder: the late high-barrier F25 wrapper still works at p = 103 once one supplies six extra complementary-support C-labels beyond the fixed C0 window.
candidate_theorem_slice: Post-97 complementary-support extension slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if one can supply p - 97 extra labels in (291, 5p + 19] whose prime support avoids {2,3,293,307,311}, then the fixed-data high-barrier wrapper with A = A0, B = {1,293,307,311}, C = C0 plus those extras, and D the complement yields a prime labeling.
next_action: Run generalize on the post-97 complementary-support supply question before any Lean work, with Γ(Z_107 × Z_25) as the next smallest discriminator if the counted supply statement still stalls.
